mechanism_narrative: A lower or flat FEDFUNDS path reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when policy rates are not rising and inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in most periods with stable or falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses predictive power once CPIAUCSL inflation is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which pressures corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS growth is also soft.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad final-demand strength that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST strength does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of jointly strong episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO while CPIAUCSL remains elevated indicates supply-side stress and margin compression, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when output weakness is matched by a rapid drop in inflation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL inflation stays above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the negative return signal vanishes when CPIAUCSL decelerates, or if triggered supply-stress episodes do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS raises expected wage income and consumption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 performance over the next week. The effect should be visible only when unemployment is not simultaneously rising sharply.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains accompanied by flat or lower UNRATE do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect reverses when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens the discount rate and signals restrictive policy, which should be followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This effect is strongest when inflation is still elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in most instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the signal disappears after conditioning on CPIAUCSL inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 signals broad real activity growth that supports earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when GDP growth is not accompanied by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 grows above its prior quarterly level
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 gains fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is unchanged or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE after a period of stable employment signals demand deterioration that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next week through earnings and risk-premium channels. The signal is regime-specific and weak when payrolls are still expanding strongly.
failure_condition: Reject if a rising UNRATE does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in the post-peak slowdown regime, or if positive payroll growth fully offsets the signal in repeated tests.
